steps.title: Use difference of squares
steps.content: Now factor the bracket: $$x^2-(x+10)^2=(x-(x+10))(x+(x+10))$$ $$=(-10)(2x+10)$$ So $2x+10$ is a factor.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The expression with root $-5$ is $2x+10$, because $2(-5)+10=0$. The other factor from the difference of squares is the constant $-10$, so $2x+10$ is the useful linear factor. Therefore, B is correct.
